activities_projects.title: Comprehensive Supply Chain Report
activities_projects.objective: Analyze all stages of shirt production and trade with real data
steps: Research costs, time, labor, and materials at each stage: cotton farming, ginning, spinning, weaving, tailoring, and retail
Prepare a detailed report including diagrams and flowcharts
Highlight social, economic, and environmental impacts at each stage
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop deep understanding of production, economics, and sustainability
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, accuracy, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Profit & Loss Analysis with Calculations
activities_projects.objective: Quantify profit distribution along the supply chain
steps: Collect or assume data for costs and selling prices at each stage
Calculate profits for farmers, workers, traders, and retailers
Analyze inequalities and suggest fairer profit-sharing methods
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand real-world economics and fairness issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of calculations and quality of analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Field Study: Local Market Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Observe and analyze local market dynamics
steps: Visit multiple local shops or markets
Interview sellers about costs, pricing, sourcing, and profits
Prepare a comparative report linking observations to chapter concepts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect theory to practice and develop research skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of observation, data accuracy, and insights (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Design an Ethical Clothing Brand
activities_projects.objective: Apply knowledge to create a socially responsible business model
materials: Chart paper or digital design tool
steps: Create a brand ensuring fair wages, safe working conditions, and sustainability
Design a brand logo, supply chain diagram, and marketing plan
Explain how it improves on traditional profit models
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop entrepreneurial thinking with social responsibility
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, feasibility, and ethical considerations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Data-Driven Debate: Who Benefits Most?
activities_projects.objective: Use evidence and analysis to discuss market inequalities
steps: Divide class into groups representing farmers, workers, traders, and retailers
Use real or assumed data to support arguments
Conduct a structured debate including ethical, social, and economic perspectives
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop reasoning, research-based arguments, and presentation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Use of data, argument quality, and teamwork (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Market Comparison Report
activities_projects.objective: Compare local and international shirt markets
steps: Research how shirts are produced and sold in India vs another country
Compare costs, wages, environmental impact, and market scale
Summarize differences in a detailed report with charts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop global awareness and analytical skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research, analysis, and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Impact of Technology on Clothing Production
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how technology changes production, labor, and pricing
steps: Research modern technologies in cotton farming, weaving, and garment making
Identify benefits and challenges for workers and businesses
Write a report highlighting changes in efficiency, cost, and labor conditions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand technological impact on society and economy
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ethical Dilemma Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Develop critical thinking about fairness in markets
steps: Create a case study showing a conflict between profit and worker welfare
Suggest possible solutions ensuring fair wages and sustainability
Discuss trade-offs and consequences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop problem-solving and ethical reasoning skills

activities_projects.title: Create a Supply Chain Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Hands-on modeling of production, trade, and pricing
steps: Design a simulation game showing how costs, profits, and supply chain decisions affect outcomes
Include unexpected events like price fluctuations or labor strikes
Play and analyze different strategies to maximize fairness and efficiency
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn systems thinking, economics, and strategy

activities_projects.title: Mini Research Paper: Clothing Market Sustainability
activities_projects.objective: Investigate environmental and social sustainability in clothing production
steps: Research environmental issues in cotton farming, textile production, and waste management
Propose sustainable practices for production and trade
Write a structured paper including references and charts
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research, critical thinking, and sustainability awareness
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth, originality, and clarity (10 marks)
